description: Baseline autoresearch harness failed locally because modal_app.py imports huggingface_hub but the local environment used by autoresearch.sh does not have it installed.
Baseline Modal sweep crashed because sweep_jobs writes /vol/runs/<sweep-id>.json without creating the parent /vol/runs directory first.
Baseline harness reached the sweep but failed when parsing the follow-up summary output; modal_app's CLI path did not emit a reliably parseable JSON payload for autoresearch.sh.

Baseline harness still failed because modal run writes its useful CLI output in a way the script was not capturing; autoresearch.sh needs to capture combined output and extract the JSON payload robustly.
Baseline harness switched to the Python Modal API but forgot to run inside modal_app.app.run(), so the remote Function was not hydrated.
Baseline Modal sweep benchmark succeeded with best_val_bpb 1.561 on the 4-job proxy grid, artifact size stayed under cap, but autoresearch.checks.sh incorrectly referenced missing main.py.
Established a working autoresearch baseline for small 1xH100 Modal architecture sweeps: 4-job grid from IDEAS.md, best val_bpb 1.5586, artifact under the 16MB cap, with fast syntax checks passing.

Adaptive short-run schedule defaults improved proxy best_val_bpb to 1.5480, but the best swept model grew to 17,051,201 bytes and violated the hard 16,000,000-byte artifact cap, so this benchmark improvement is not acceptable.
Re-baselined the Modal proxy sweep after fixing ranking to enforce the hard 16,000,000-byte artifact cap; best valid run is 1.5604 val_bpb with constraint_ok=1.

Switched the Modal proxy environment to Python 3.14 and updated train_gpt compression to use stdlib zstd when available (fallback zlib otherwise), reducing compressed artifact size to 9.09MB and slightly improving constrained best_val_bpb to 1.5593.

Adaptive short-run warmdown and optimizer warmup defaults under zstd compression made the constrained sweep clearly worse (1.5703 val_bpb) while staying under the cap, so the schedule adaptation does not help this proxy.

Replacing the relu^2 MLP activation with silu^2 shrank the compressed artifact slightly but made constrained sweep quality worse (1.5631 val_bpb), so the smoother activation is not helping this workload.

Reducing the default qk_gain_init from 1.5 to 1.0 slightly worsened constrained sweep quality (1.5629 val_bpb) while leaving size comfortably under cap, so the original q/k gain is better for this proxy.

Inspired by PR #250, tested cosine warm restarts/SGDR on the constrained 4-job Modal proxy sweep. It was clearly worse (1.5692 val_bpb) than the current zstd baseline, so this schedule does not transfer cleanly to the short proxy.
